_strategy: R8 confirmed: only currency solo PodFailure is a stable +1 (3 confirmations). All other otel-demo signals are flaky/single-shot. R9 strategy: (1) Aggressive currency stability sweep — 5x consecutive solo runs to get confidence interval. (2) Test currency with different chaos types (PodKill, NetworkLoss80 outbound, MemoryStress with int MiB, JVMException since currency is Java). (3) Re-test fraud-detection×image-provider PAIR to see if it was really emergent or noise. (4) Force pair-prob=0 since pairing dilutes currency signal.
_results: currency stability sweep: x02 -1, x03 +1, x08 +1 → 2/3 valid solo PodFailure runs +1. With prior R6+1, R7v01+1, R8w10+1, currency is roughly 5/6 ≈ 83% hit rate. x04 currency/PodKill -1 (different chaos). x07 image-provider solo +1 NEW (solo flipped from R8 -1 to R9 +1 — flaky). x06 fraud-detection solo -1 (confirmed: only fires in pair). x10 product-catalog -1 fault.injection.failed (env). x01/x09/x05 submission anomalies (null ns or 500). **Conclusion**: currency is the most-stable signal; image-provider is flaky (~50%); fraud-detection alone never fires; pair (R7) was an artifact. Detector signal in otel-demo has high run-to-run variance.
